---
title: "Editable Extension for Quarto Revealjs"
---

This Revealjs plugin allows the repositioning, resizing, and rotating of images and text divs directly in the previewed slides.

## Try It Out

Click inside the embedded presentation below to interact with the editable elements:

```{=html}
<div style="position: relative; width: 100%; padding-bottom: 56.25%; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden;">
  <iframe src="example.html" style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; border: none;"></iframe>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-note}
In this demo, the Save button will download a file. In your own projects with `quarto preview`, it will save changes directly back to your `.qmd` file.
:::

Or [open in a new tab](example.html){target="_blank"} for the full experience.

## Key Features

- **Move** elements by dragging them to reposition
- **Resize** elements using corner handles (hold Shift to preserve aspect ratio)
- **Rotate** elements using the orange circular handle (hold Shift to snap to 15° increments)
- **Add arrows** with straight or curved (Bezier) paths via [quarto-arrows](https://github.com/EmilHvitfeldt/quarto-arrows) integration
- **Rich text editing** with formatting toolbar
- **Brand color support** via Quarto's `_brand.yml`
- **Keyboard navigation** for accessibility
- **Undo/Redo** support

## Quick Start

Install the extension:

```bash
quarto add emilhvitfeldt/quarto-revealjs-editable
```

Add to your YAML header:

```yaml
revealjs-plugins:
  - editable
filters:
  - editable
```

Mark elements as editable:

```markdown
![](image.png){.editable}

::: {.editable}
some text here
:::
```

See the [Getting Started](getting-started.qmd) guide for more details.

## Demo

![](media/demo-text.mp4)
